title: Twitter at Twenty: Commemoration and Commiseration (M/C Journal issue mentioned on Snurblog)
snippet: Fabio Giglietto is listed as one of the contributing social media researchers to a special issue of M/C Journal, announced on Axel Bruns' blog in April 2026, commemorating the 20th anniversary of Twitter.
description: The mention is an announcement on Axel Bruns' blog about a special M/C Journal issue on Twitter's 20th anniversary, listing Fabio Giglietto as a contributing social media researcher.

title: Referendum. Bonelli e Fratoianni (Avs) scrivono al Presidente Agcom, preoccupati per le fake news veicolate sui social. Chiesto incontro. Piccolotti: inquietante che tecnologie opache abbiano cosi tanto spazio sui social del nostro Paese, con operazioni di propaganda finalizzate a condizionare l'opinione pubblica e fondate sul falso
snippet: This news article from March 3, 2026, reports on a letter sent to the President of AGCOM by politicians Bonelli and Fratoianni (Avs), expressing concerns about fake news on social media. The letter cites a study by Professor Fabio Giglietto (University of Urbino) titled 'Meta's Political Content Reduction Policy and Italian Parliamentarians' Facebook Visibility,' which highlights how Meta's algorithmic filter has contributed to the spread of right-wing extremist voices at the expense of parliamentarians.
description: A news article from March 2026 reports on political concerns about fake news on social media, citing a study by Professor Fabio Giglietto (University of Urbino) titled 'Meta's Political Content Reduction Policy and Italian Parliamentarians' Facebook Visibility.' The study highlights how Meta's algorithmic filter impacts political discourse by favoring right-wing extremist voices.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: The article reports on a study by the University of Urbino, co-authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto, which found that Facebook's algorithm penalizes institutional political visibility and promotes anti-European, pro-Russian, and conspiracist extremist content.
description: The article reports on a study co-authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino, which found that Facebook's algorithm penalizes institutional political visibility and promotes anti-European, pro-Russian, and conspiracist extremist content.

snippet: Fabio Giglietto is listed as an organizer and a member of the Program Committee for the CS2Italy conference, taking place from June 13th-15th, 2026.
